name: Doctrine Layer
definition: Non-negotiable creative, ethical, and architectural constraints.

name: Workspace / Brand Layer
definition: Brand ownership, identity, consent, voice, visual DNA, and workspace state.

name: Primitive System Layer
definition: Meaning and experience primitives, coalitions, edge products, primitive evals.

name: Methodology / Reasoning Layer
definition: Reusable reasoning doctrines: CBAR, CCV, RSCS, PRIMAL, VAE, SDA, SFL.

name: Research Layer
definition: Guest, audience, visual, asset, source, rights, and performance research.

name: Interview Intelligence Layer
definition: Interview briefs, contracts, anchors, expression sessions, and ingredient acquisition.

name: Content Strategy Layer
definition: Archetypes, derivatives, packs, content asset codes, reaction/meme mechanisms.

name: Sequencing Layer
definition: Viewer-state ordering, open loops, closure contracts, and package sequences.

name: Creative Ingredient Layer
definition: Source-reference-bound visual ingredients, style routes, frame profiles, composition roles.

name: Asset Intelligence Layer
definition: Scored assets, libraries, provenance, usage, winners, and fatigue.

name: Visual Style / Motion Skill Layer
definition: CAC, GMG, paper-cut, documentary, UI reaction, and avatar style routes.

name: Visual Pre-Production Layer
definition: Visual Schema, Storyboard Composer, PRIMAL, VAE, T/V-Codes, Visual Analyst.

name: Composition Layer
definition: Scene/slide/still/video templates, layer plans, frame profiles, composition receipts.

name: Component / Engine Layer
definition: SuperVisual, Carousel, Video, 2D Character, Sequencing, Asset, and Brand engines.

name: Provider / Tool Layer
definition: External/local AI models and deterministic/rendering/media tools.

name: Render / Timeline Layer
definition: Render contracts, timeline manifests, captions, audio, EDL/OTIO, receipts.

name: Evaluation / Review Layer
definition: Eval receipts, review states, approval blockers, repair commands, Telegram review.

name: Publishing / Memory Layer
definition: Publishing intents, Publer jobs, performance telemetry, narrative memory, winning libraries.
